title: A Feasibility Study on Biometric Identification Using Multimodal Signal Analysis of Photoplethysmogram and Phonocardiogram
venue: 2024 International Conference on Smart Healthcare and Wearable Intelligent Devices (SHWID 2024)

method.training_strategy: Hold-out method (8:2 training/test split), Adam optimizer, cross-entropy loss

evaluation_protocol: Stratified sampling for hold-out validation (80% training, 20% testing).

reported_limitations: Need for more diverse cardiac signals

usability.usability_issues: Requirement to press the device closely against the chest

key_contribution: Demonstrated that multimodal fusion of PPG and PCG signals improves biometric identification accuracy compared to unimodal signals
Compared the performance of a specialized CNN-LSTM architecture (Modified-BiometricNet) against ResNet18 for cardiac signal identification
main_strength: Use of multimodal cardiac signals to reduce overfitting and enhance feature extraction
main_weakness: Lack of evaluation using standard biometric metrics like EER or FAR
Lab-only data collection with controlled subject movement
insights.notes_for_review: The paper reports only Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1, which are excluded from the primary evaluation extraction per guidelines. However, the fusion benefit is clearly quantified via Accuracy.
